PIRA-Bench

A Transition from Reactive GUI Agents to
GUI-based Proactive Intent Recommendation Agents

Yuxiang Chai, Shunye Tang, Han Xiao, Rui Liu, Hongsheng Li

MMLab @ CUHK  |  Nankai University  |  Huawei Research

Shhh, these are your private notes 📝

The Paradigm Shift: Reactive to Proactive

From instruction-following to intent-anticipation

Current GUI agents operate under a reactive paradigm, requiring explicit, detailed instructions to execute tasks.

  • The Limitation: Users must meticulously prompt the agent, increasing cognitive burden and often forgetting context.
  • The Solution: Proactive Intent Recommendation (PIR) agents.
  • How it works: Continuously monitors visual streams, anticipates latent goals amidst noise, and autonomously proposes actionable tasks.

Introducing PIRA-Bench

The first benchmark for Proactive Intent Recommendation

Complex Trajectories

100 curated real-world GUI trajectories (avg. 33 screenshots). Agents must successfully disentangle concurrent, interleaved user activities.

Deep Personalization

3 distinct user profiles per trajectory. Agents cross-reference visual cues with socio-economic status to generate customized intents.

Noise & Hallucination

Universal noise injection simulates clutter. Includes "pure noise" samples to rigorously test an agent's ability to remain safely idle.

The PIRF Baseline

Proactive Intent Recommendation Framework

A state-tracking cognitive architecture designed for continuous intent disentanglement and reflection.

  • Dynamic Memory: Anchors static user profiles while tracking an active "intent bank".
  • Action Space: At each frame, the MLLM outputs a structured transition: CREATE, RESUME, UPDATE, or IDLE.
  • Auto-Deletion: Continuously reflects on visual evidence to purge abandoned tasks and prevent memory bloat.

Rigorous Evaluation

Measuring Proactivity and Restraint

Intent F1 Score

Assesses model accuracy on trajectories with actionable intents, comparing semantic alignment of predicted vs. ground truth intents.

Normalized FPS

Evaluates noise robustness on negative samples. Strictly penalizes hallucinations when no actionable intent exists.

Final Score

The product of F1 and FPS. Ensures top-performing agents master complex prediction while maintaining operational restraint.

Experimental Results

Overcoming the "Over-Proactivity" Trap

  • The Naive Baseline: Frontier models (e.g., GPT-5.2) show high recall but suffer from severe hallucinations during idle moments.
  • The "Trigger-Happy" Flaw: Without memory and reflection, models misinterpret noise as meaningful signals.
  • PIRF Superiority: Structured state tracking drastically improves precision (+18.5%) and noise robustness (+12.5%).
  • Human Benchmark: Humans demonstrate near-perfect precision (98.7%) and noise robustness (96.2%).

The Distraction Cost

Ablation studies reveal current MLLMs are highly susceptible to visual distraction.

  • Clean Trajectories: High capability (F1 > 74%)
  • Noised Trajectories: Drastic collapse in precision (drops > 40%)

Conclusion: Future agents must not only be smarter, but master the discernment to remain silent.

Thank You
